summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirmed that comment data, processing-instruction data, and processing-instruction targets can escape their intended XML boundary and materialize attacker-injected elements after serialization and reparsing.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should produce serialized XML in which attacker-controlled comment and processing-instruction payloads break out into real `<injected .../>` elements, proving XML structure injection rather than a generic parse error.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned xmldom at tag 0.8.12, built the reproduction image, and printed `COMMENT_INJECTION_CONFIRMED`, `PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED`, and `XMLSERIALIZER_COMMENT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED` after reparsing the serialized output and observing injected elements in both comment and PI cases.
verification_method: The verification helper runs a Node harness inside the container, serializes crafted comment and processing-instruction payloads with `requireWellFormed: true`, reparses the resulting XML, and asserts that injected elements are present. The helper emits distinct proof strings only when the injected elements are actually observed.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
